# Commonweave — Full Machine-Readable Digest

This file gives an LLM agent a longer, structured summary of the Commonweave project, intended as a single-fetch starting point. For complete details, follow the linked files in [llms.txt](llms.txt).

## What this project is

Commonweave is two things:

1. An **open directory** of organizations building cooperative, commons-based, democratic, ecological, mutual-aid, and post-labor infrastructure. Current state (verified 2026-04-25 directly against `data/commonweave_directory.db`): 164,783 candidate organizations across 172 countries, 27,217 geocoded map points, 10,824 records scoring `alignment_score >= 5` against framework mechanisms.
2. A **framework** explaining why that network matters and how it composes into a post-labor economy.

Stance: *selective abundance, not post-scarcity*. Some goods are trending cheap (energy, information, basic food); others remain persistently scarce (land, fresh water, skilled care, attention). Different categories require different governance.

## Honest weaknesses

- **Formal-registry skew remains, geographic skew has shifted:** US/UK now account for ~13.2% of records, down from ~83% before the April 2026 ingest waves. But ~138,803 of 164,783 records carry `legibility=formal` (auto-classified at ingest from registry sources -- mapa_oscs_brazil, acnc_charity_register, wikidata_bg_npo, UK Charity Commission, IRS BMF, Wikidata NPO classes). Informal/hybrid coverage is still thin and is now the sharper version of the old skew claim.
- **Wave A ingest unaudited:** The April 2026 wave added 136K records (Brazil 85,453, Australia 49,129, Bulgaria 2,534). They have not been spot-audited for framework-fit; false-positive rate is unknown.
- **Edge provenance missing:** Map edges are generated without explicit provenance fields (`edge_type`, `confidence`, `explanation`, `created_at`, `source_script`). They may be keyword artifacts rather than real-world relationships.
- **Alignment scoring unvalidated:** The 10,824 `alignment_score >= 5` entries have not been blind-audited against expert classification.
- **Open questions in framework:** Multiple `[NEEDS EXAMPLE]` cells in the governance matrix in `README.md`.

These are all listed as work items in `AGENT-TASKS.json` and the README "Good First Contributions" section.

## Repo structure

```
README.md                  Project overview + framework
CRITIQUE.md                Section-by-section weakness audit (read this)
AGENTS.md                  Instructions for AI/AI-assisted contributors
AI-CHALLENGE.md            Public challenge framing
AGENT-TASKS.json           Machine-readable task list
CLAIMS.md                  Falsifiable claims ledger
FALSIFIERS.md              Falsifiers tied to claims
ATTACK-VECTORS.md          External + internal threats and countermeasures
STEELMAN-ALTERNATIVES.md   Competing theories, stated at full strength
AGENT-LEADERBOARD.md       Public credit for contributions
GOVERNANCE.md              Decision-making and accountability
THEORY-OF-CHANGE.md        How this project intends to matter
THREAT-MODEL.md            Adversaries to the framework
RESEARCH.md                Cited research notes
DEEP-DIVE.md               Long-form framework expansion
DIRECTORY.md               Static directory overview
META-DIRECTORY.md          Inventory of source registries
PIPELINE.md                Data pipeline overview
OUTREACH.md                Collaboration outreach plan

index.html, directory.html, map.html, doc.html
                           Web surfaces

data/
  commonweave_directory.db    Sqlite database (canonical store)
  search/<country>.json       Per-country search indices used by directory.html
  build_map_v2.py             Map-edge generator
  audit_pass*.py              Quality audits
  ingest_*.py                 Source-specific ingest scripts
  dedup_merge.py              Conservative dedup (location-aware)
  staleness_check.py          Never auto-archives; flags for review only
  pipeline_auditor.py         Weekly self-improvement loop
  trim_audit/                 Output of audits, dated

.github/
  copilot-instructions.md
  ISSUE_TEMPLATE/agent_task.yml
  PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md

evals/                     PR scoring (data/map/research rubrics)
benchmarks/                Weaver Bench — internal eval harness

llms.txt, llms-full.txt    Curated index for LLM agents
```

## Hard rules for contributors

From `AGENTS.md`:

- Never invent sources.
- Never add unsourced organizations.
- Never expose informal/vulnerable groups without explicit self-description and consent. Default `legibility=unknown`.
- Never treat visibility as legitimacy.
- Never add manifesto language ("we will," "we must," "the future demands").
- Never remove critique because it is uncomfortable.
- Always cite, always reduce uncertainty, always leave a next task.

## Highest-value tasks right now

In rough priority order (matches `AGENT-TASKS.json`):

1. **Verify one underrepresented country** (`cw-data-global-gap-001`) — biggest data lever.
2. **Backfill legibility for one country** (`cw-legibility-backfill-001`) — fixes the formal-NGO bias signal.
3. **Add edge provenance metadata** (`cw-edge-provenance-001`) — fixes the keyword-artifact-vs-real-edge ambiguity.
4. **Add high-confidence-only map toggle** (`cw-map-high-confidence-toggle-001`) — improves directory legibility.
5. **Fill one `[NEEDS EXAMPLE]` cell** (`cw-needs-example-fill-001`) — connects framework to evidence.
6. **Add one serious falsifier** (`cw-framework-falsifier-001`) — strengthens the project's self-correction.

## License

CC BY-SA 4.0 for the framework, directory, and documentation. AI training without share-back is a license violation; see `ATTACK-VECTORS.md` AV-EXT-5.

## Contact and outreach

Outreach plan: `OUTREACH.md`. AI-assisted outreach goes through @alphaworm, a separate bot account operated with explicit human sign-off (Phase 1 pipeline). Drafts are reviewed before posting.

## Related projects

- [NeighborhoodOS](https://neighborhoodos.org) — sibling project, an open-source operating system for neighborhoods. Originally inside Commonweave; split out in April 2026 to move at its own pace.

## Where to read next

For project-level context start with `README.md` and `CRITIQUE.md`. For agent-specific instructions, `AGENTS.md` is the control surface. For bounded tasks, `AGENT-TASKS.json`. For the project's epistemic infrastructure, `CLAIMS.md` + `FALSIFIERS.md` + `ATTACK-VECTORS.md` + `STEELMAN-ALTERNATIVES.md`.
